title: Chief Clarence Alexander receives the Presidential Citizens Medal
summary: Clarence Alexander received the the second highest civilian honor from President Obama last week. In a ceremony at the White House, Alexander of Fort Yukon, Alaska was awarded the Presidential Citizens Medal for his contributions to his community. title: U.S. Dept. of Energy seeks Alaska Native interns
summary: The U.S. Department of Energy is accepting applications for Alaska Native and Native American college students to support tribal energy program efforts as 2012 summer interns. Interns will gain hands-on experience working on renewable energy projects in Arizona, California, New Mexico and Utah. Lear...

title: Apply online to TCF
summary: Since 1982, The CIRI Foundation (TCF) has provided more than $19 million in scholarship and grant assistance to support original enrollees of CIRI and their direct lineal descendants in the pursuit of postsecondary education. Looking ahead to the future and with the goal of streamlining the applicat...

title: Words have power to drive change
summary: Introduction May we live our lives so that Our Words Have Power. Good Evening elders, youth, delegates and guests. My name is Ethan Schutt. I am the grandson of Josephine and the late Lawrence Roberts of Tanana. I was born to Jeanette Roberts/Scannell and am the son of David and the late Joyce Schutt. Many of you may know my twin brother, Aaron, who is now the President and CEO of Doyon, Limited, the regional corporation for our Interior River villages. I am also joined here tonight by my wife Kristina and our children. I am honored to speak to you as we close our 2011 AFN Convention. Our theme this week has been “Strength In Unity”. It is a message with strong meaning and resonance for us as we attempt to navigate the challenges of our modern world. We are diverse in culture, geography and background. We represent tribes, villages and Alaska Native Corporations. We...

title: Fifer wins September stock will prize
summary: The winner for the September 2011 stock will participation prize is Larry F. Fifer II. To be eligible for the monthly $200 prize drawings, shareholders must have a will disposing of their CIRI shares on file in CIRI’s Shareholder Relations Department. title: Evans Smith named ANHC head
summary: The Alaska Native Heritage Center (ANHC) board of directors has chosen Annette Evans Smith as new president and CEO. Evans Smith replaces CIRI shareholder Jon Ross, who completed his contract in early June. Evans Smith joined ANHC as vice president of community relations and development in 2003. She...

summary: The National Indian Health Board (NIHB) presented CIRI shareholder Roy Huhndorf and Southcentral Foundation’s (SCF) Responsible Adolescents in Successful Employment (RAISE) program with awards at its 28th Annual Consumer Conference in September. title: CIHA’s Carol Gore wins national housing and redevelopment award
summary: Cook Inlet Housing Authority (CIHA) President and CEO Carol Gore is the 2011 recipient of the prestigious M. title: CIRI honors Elder Shareholders and Shareholder of the Year
summary: CIRI honored three Kenai sisters as Elder Shareholders of the Year and the head of Cook Inlet Native Head Start as Shareholder of the Year. title: Shareholder receives AFN award
summary: CIRI shareholder Loretta Throop earned an Alaska Federation of Natives (AFN) President’s Award for her longtime commitment to bettering the physical and mental health of Alaska Native people. title: Baby Ruby Mae
summary: CIRI shareholder Keri Bartlett proudly announces the birth of her granddaughter, Ruby Mae. She is the daughter of CIRI descendant Jussi Ann Phenis and her husband, Alan. title: Little league all star
summary: CIRI descendant Ryan Baird Galuz earned a spot on the Eastlake Little League All Star team, for the second year in a row. title: CIRI Spotlight:  Christopher Dahl
summary: “I had to pinch myself the first time I had a layover in Honolulu,” CIRI shareholder and Alaska Airlines pilot Christopher Dahl reminisced. “All that hard work and there I was.” Dahl, who is Yup’ik and Aleut, has been a commercial airline pilot for 11 years. title: CIRI among top Alaska companies
summary: CIRI ranks 19 on Alaska Business Monthly’s 2011 list of the top 49 revenue-producing Alaska-owned and-operated companies, having posted $188,357,000 in revenue in 2010. title: CIRI to conduct seismic tests for Stone Horn Ridge UCG project
summary: CIRI will conduct high-resolution seismic tests to complete geologic modeling for an underground coal gasification (UCG) project being developed by Stone Horn Ridge, a company owned by CIRI and Houston-based Laurus Energy. title: A word from the president:  AFN evolving to unify, better serve
summary: By: Margie Brown, CIRI president and CEO Two years ago, as a delegate to the Alaska Federation of Natives (AFN) Convention, I was pleased to vote to approve resolution 09-05, a resolution directing the AFN board to undertake a process to reorganize AFN. title: Shareholder remembers her father’s ANCSA efforts
summary: Darlene Wright remembers exactly where she was 40 years ago when the Alaska Native Claims Settlement Act (ANCSA) became law. title: RCA approves Fire Island deal
summary: The Regulatory Commission of Alaska (RCA) approved Chugach Electric Association’s power purchase agreement to buy Fire Island wind power on Oct. 10.
